channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Firefighters report progress against LA blazes as winds pick up again
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Millions of Southern Californians nervously kept watch as winds began picking up Wednesday during a final round of dangerous wildfire weather forecast for the region where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 people and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hope and dread as Israelis and Palestinians await a Gaza truce that may not end their suffering
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — With a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas tantalizingly close, families of hostages in the Gaza Strip are afraid to get their hopes up and agonizing over the unknown.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, SAM MEDNICK and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As Los Angeles burns, Hollywood's Oscar season turns into a pledge drive
item.description: When the Palisades Fire broke out in Los Angeles last Tuesday, Hollywood’s awards season was in full swing. The Golden Globes had transpired less than 48 hours earlier and a series of splashy awards banquets followed in the days after.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The long struggle to establish Martin Luther King Jr. Day
item.description: The Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his iconic “I Have a Dream” speech in 1963 on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial. He chose that location in part to honor President Abraham Lincoln as “a great American, in whose symbolic shadow we stand today.” Now, millions of people honor King in the same way.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire tornadoes are a risk under California's extreme wildfire conditions
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter fire tornadoes — a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to Chicago after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires burning homes and businesses in Los Angeles for a week have killed at least 25 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 buildings in what might be the most expensive set of conflagrations in the nation's history. The blazes started Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to O'Hare after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Fire tornadoes a risk under California's extreme wildfire conditions
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass posed for photos at a cocktail party as Palisades Fire exploded
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass is at the center of controversy after social media photos showed her posing at a cocktail party in Ghana as the Palisades Fire exploded.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FDA proposes sweeping limits on nicotine in cigarettes
item.description: It's an 11th-hour plan from the Biden administration that will take years to go into effect, if it happens at all.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New documentary reveals antisemitic hostage siege captured on camera at Texas synagogue
item.description: Unseen security footage from the 11-hour Colleyville synagogue hostage crisis, capturing terror and resilience, is shown for the first time in a new documentary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Craft retailer Joann files for bankruptcy
item.description: Craft retailer Joann filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on Wednesday morning, marking the second time the company has done so in a year.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Relief, 21st-century style: As wildfires burn, GoFundMe becomes a repository of harrowing stories
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They seem endless, these sapping stories of loss. A grandfather starts over in his 90s. A family loses their dream home. People who were already struggling are dealt new, brutal blows.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA floats plan to make cigarettes nonaddictive, but its fate rests with Trump
item.description: The Biden administration is floating a long-awaited proposal to make cigarettes less addictive by capping their nicotine levels.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: SpaceX has launched a pair of lunar landers for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business up there.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AAPI adults prioritize immigration, but split on mass deportations: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration is an issue that many Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders want the government to focus on this year, but they are sharply divided on what actions President-elect Donald Trump should take when he returns to the White House, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: Preston Martin figured the retro blue Volkswagen van he slept in for a year during college was a goner, given that he parked it in a Malibu neighborhood just before the Palisades fire ripped through, reducing homes and cars to rubble and charred metal.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House barrels toward fight over placing conditions on California fire aid
item.description: The House is barreling toward a contentious debate over disaster relief for California as many — but not all — Republicans call for conditions to be placed on the assistance, an idea Democrats are soundly rejecting. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Man pleads guilty in string of MS-13 killings that stunned New York suburbs
item.description: CENTRAL ISLIP, N.Y. (AP) — A man who helped lead an MS-13 clique in New York pleaded guilty Tuesday in a federal racketeering case involving seven murders, including the 2016 killings of two high school girls that focused the nation’s attention on the violent Central American street gang.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dementia cases expected to double by 2060: Study
item.description: Dementia cases in the U.S. are expected to double by 2060 when around 1 million Americans are projected to develop the memory-losing condition every year, according to a new study published Monday in the medical journal Nature Medicine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: This popular hiking trail may hold clues to how Palisades Fire ignited
item.description: A beloved hiking spot north of Sunset Boulevard in Pacific Palisades is reportedly a point of interest for investigators looking into the origins of the costliest wildfire in the history of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Abbott: Flags will be at full staff outside Texas buildings on Inauguration Day
item.description: Gov. Gregg Abbott announced on Monday he will order flags outside all state buildings to be at half staff on Jan. 20 in honor of Inauguration Day, according to a news release from the governor's office.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, Fox 5

item.title: Oscars push back nominations announcement amid Calif. wildfires
item.description: With fires still active in the Los Angeles area, the film academy also extended the nominations voting period for its members through Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, ABC 4

item.title: 24 dead as Los Angeles fires leave destruction, devastation in wake; strong winds expected this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled Sunday to make further progress against wildfires that have destroyed thousands of homes and killed 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: IRS delays after ID thefts 'unconscionable,' watchdog says. Here are 3 ways to protect yourself
item.description: A new tax season is nearly here, and with it an opportunity for identity thieves to take advantage of taxpayers.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: 16 dead, more than a dozen missing as crews continue fighting blazes
item.description: More than a dozen people have died as a result of the still-spreading wildfires burning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5
